title: Eleocharis obtusetrigona
taxonomy: Eleocharis obtusetrigona is classified as a species of sedge, which falls under the family Cyperaceae. The genus Eleocharis is a group of sedges that are commonly found in wetland environments. Eleocharis obtusetrigona is further classified as a monocotyledonous plant, meaning it has one cotyledon or seed leaf in its seedling stage. Within the kingdom Plantae, Eleocharis obtusetrigona is classified as a flowering plant, and it belongs to the division Magnoliophyta. The classification of Eleocharis obtusetrigona is rounded out by its rank as a species, which is a taxonomic level below the genus.

intro: Eleocharis obtusetrigona, commonly known as the obtuse trigonous spike-rush, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The health benefits of Eleocharis obtusetrigona include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
